mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and compress equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns in >55% of periods following rate increases, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 4.5 percent signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending expectations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in >60% of instances when UNRATE crosses above 4.5 percent, or signal Sharpe ratio is negative over 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand that supports corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to generate positive 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above 0.5 percent monthly confirms supply-side capacity utilization that drives equity valuations higher over the next 20 trading days during disinflationary periods.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth above 0.5 percent yields negative 20-day returns in >55% of observations when CPIAUCSL growth is below 3 percent annually.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 2 percent annualized confirms economic expansion that lifts equity prices, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2 percent, or out-of-sample hit rate drops below 0.45.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly CPI inflation surpassing 8 percent forces aggressive central bank tightening that compresses equity multiples, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in >50% of months when CPIAUCSL exceeds 240, or cumulative drawdown does not exceed 3 percent within the horizon.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total employment combined with falling unemployment indicates labor market tightening that boosts wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline produces negative 20-day returns in >55% of cases, or interaction effect fails significance testing at 90% confidence.

mechanism_narrative: Falling retail sales during a monetary tightening cycle signals demand destruction that outweighs rate hike pricing, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of periods when RSAFS declines while FEDFUNDS rises, or signal alpha is negative after transaction costs.
